name: tracemind-instrumentation
updatePolicy: Check tracemind.agent_guidance before changing TraceMind instrumentation. If local Skill, AGENTS rules, or manifest content may be stale, call tracemind.check_agent_setup and merge only missing guidance after telling the user which files will change. In multi-project setups, use tracemind.project_info or MCP tool metadata to confirm the project before adding events. Daily operations review uses dashboard-aligned project_health/recent_online before instrumentation setup; use tracemind.project_health to surface SDK upgrade findings, daily health, Dashboard-aligned trends, agent setup update reminders, and current attention items before changing instrumentation. Ask the user before updating local skill or instruction files. Ask before calling tracemind.submit_feedback unless the developer explicitly requested submission. If the developer asks whether you can directly feedback to TraceMind, search for and use tracemind.submit_feedback instead of concluding from a partial active tool list that no feedback tool exists. Use captureError for privacy-safe app_error summaries. Use submitFeedback, query_user_feedback, and update_user_feedback for terminal user feedback workflows.
sdkUpgradePolicy: For SDK platforms, capture_setup returns latestSdk, installedVersionDetection, upgradePolicy, upgradeCommands, and verificationCommands. Use registry install commands when distributionMode is registry; use localSourceFallback or local_source instructions only when needed. Fetch source from latestSdk.sourceRef exactly for local-source installs; release builds use immutable tracemind-release-<version> tags instead of floating main. Compare sourceDetails.sdkContentHash against latestSdk.contentHash; displayVersion is only human-readable.
